expectations.text: Marks task T003 as [X] or completed because a clean TDD RED failure is a successful test-writing outcome
expectations.evidence: Response says `Mark T003 as [X]` and `This counts as a verified TDD RED step`.

expectations.text: Distinguishes between a clean RED failure (tests fail for the intended reason) and a broken test (syntax errors, import crashes)
expectations.evidence: Response distinguishes the outcome as `clean RED; the implementation does not exist yet, and the failures are for the intended reason`.

expectations.text: References TDD-first or RED-GREEN workflow as the reason this counts as success
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly references the `TDD RED step` and verified RED outcome as the basis for success.

expectations.text: Does NOT treat the test failures as an error that blocks progress
expectations.evidence: Response says `Continue to the next task; the implementation work remains for a later phase/task`, so the failures do not block progress.

claims.claim: Expected test failures can still complete a test-writing task when they are a clean RED result.
claims.evidence: The response marks T003 complete because all four failures are for the intended missing-implementation reason.
